titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Maureen Stapleton Papers
titlestmt.subtitle: Stapleton (Maureen) Papers
titlestmt.author: by Rosemary K. J. Davis and Caroline Pryor

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday May 15, 2019 at 03:58

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on the American Literature Fund, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Correspondence and personal papers, 1937-2006. II. Printed material, 1947-1997. III. Photographs and film stills, 1935-1992. IV. Scripts, 1958-2006. V. Awards and certificates, 1967-2000.

bioghist.head: Maureen Stapleton
bioghist.p: Maureen Stapleton (1925-2006) was an award-winning American actress. Born in Troy, New York, Stapleton acted in theater, television, and films. She won two Tony Awards, received an Best Supporting Actress Oscar for her work in Reds (1981), and earned an Emmy for Among the Paths of Eden (1968). Stapleton died in 2006.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Maureen Stapleton Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Maureen Stapleton Papers, which span from 1935 to 2006, contain extensive correspondence with actors, directors and authors including Elizabeth Taylor and Tennessee Williams. Also contains printed material, photographs of Stapleton and other actors, annotated scripts, and other papers by or relating to Maureen Stapleton's life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Maureen Stapleton Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

subject.part: Motion picture actors and actresses -- United States -- 20th Century
Television actors and actresses -- United States

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence to and from Maureen Stapleton including letters from Helen Hayes, Elizabeth Taylor, Mike Nichols, and other actors, directors, and individuals.

did.unittitle: "Bouquets a la Carte" card from Elizabeth Taylor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with Stapleton's friends, peers, and colleagues including a telegram and letter from Grace Kelly, personal correspondence with members of the Kennedy family, and a telegram invite to Robert F. Kennedy's requiem mass.

did.unittitle: Siena College and Troy, New York material
Photograph of Maureen Stapleton with Neil Simon
Film still of Stapleton in "For Whom the Bell Tolls"
State of NY legislation document regarding death of Maureen Stapleton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a copy of Maureen Stapleton's funeral program.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains books from Maureen Stapleton's personal library, including some volumes with annotations and inscriptions.

did.unittitle: When Do I Start? A Memoir
Detective Story: A Play in Three Acts
Sardi's: The Story of a Famous Restaurant
Me: A Modern Master's Book for Children

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains portraits, publicity images, and candid photographs of Maureen Stapleton and other individuals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Helen Hayes, Spangler Arlington Brugh, Errol Flynn, Barbra Streisand, and Natalie Wood.

did.unittitle: Photographs from "Cat on a Hot Tin Roof"
Photograph album featuring colleagues and film career
Photograph album featuring Elizabeth Taylor and Tennessee Williams
Photograph album featuring photos of Stapleton and friends

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain organization found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: "Child, Childbirth, Childhood, Children" by William Saroyan
"Broadway" by Philip Dunning and George Abbott
Letters to Kathy Bamberry regarding Stapleton's death
"A Second Epiphany for my Friend Maureen" by Tennessee Williams

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains awards, nomination certificates, and other documentation primarily related to Maureen Stapleton's work as an actress.

did.unittitle: Emmy Award "Outstanding Single Performance by an Actress in a Leading Role in a Drama" nomination for "Among the Paths to Eden"
Drama Desk Award for "The Gingerbread Lady"
"Best Spoken Word, Documentary or Drama Recording" Grammy nomination for "To Kill a Mockingbird"
Honorary degree from Russell Sage College
Charter Member Certificate for Hollywood Stuntmen's Hall of Fame
Golden Globe Best Supporting Actress nomination for "Interiors"
Antoinette Perry Award nomination for "The Little Foxes"
Dedication plaque for Maureen Stapleton Theater
Best Supporting Actress Oscar nomination for "Airport"
Certificate of recognition for "Maureen Stapleton Day"
Los Angeles Film Critics Association Best Supporting Actress award for "Interiors"
Best Actress in a Supporting Role Certificate of Nomination for "Interiors"
Award for Best Supporting Actress in "Red" from the Los Angeles Film Critics Association
Annual Award for Best Supporting Actress in "Reds," from 1981 National Society of Film Critics
Award of Honor for Arts and Culture
